nutrient_interactions.effect: Calcium inhibits non-haem iron absorption when taken concurrently at doses above 300 mg calcium.
nutrient_interactions.recommendation: Separate calcium and iron supplements by at least 2 hours.
references: Hallberg L et al. Am J Clin Nutr. 1991;53(1):112-119.

nutrient_interactions.effect: High-dose zinc supplementation (>50 mg/day) competitively inhibits copper absorption and can cause copper deficiency.
nutrient_interactions.recommendation: Ensure copper intake is adequate when supplementing zinc at high doses. A zinc:copper ratio of 8–15:1 is generally considered safe.
references: Fosmire GJ. Am J Clin Nutr. 1990;51(2):225-227.

nutrient_interactions.effect: Calcium and magnesium compete for the same intestinal absorption pathway. Very high calcium intake may reduce magnesium absorption.
nutrient_interactions.recommendation: Take calcium and magnesium supplements at different times of day if doses are high.
references: Hardwick LL et al. Am J Clin Nutr. 1991;53(3):762-766.

nutrient_interactions.effect: Vitamin D significantly enhances intestinal calcium absorption. Adequate vitamin D is necessary for calcium to be absorbed effectively.
nutrient_interactions.recommendation: Ensure adequate vitamin D when supplementing calcium. This is a beneficial synergistic interaction.
references: Heaney RP. J Steroid Biochem Mol Biol. 2005;97(1-2):13-19.

nutrient_interactions.effect: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) substantially enhances non-haem iron absorption by reducing ferric iron to the more absorbable ferrous form.
nutrient_interactions.recommendation: Taking iron with vitamin C is beneficial and commonly recommended to improve iron absorption.
references: Hallberg L et al. Scand J Gastroenterol Suppl. 1987;129:93-99.

medication_interactions.effect: Vitamin K is the direct antagonist of warfarin. Consistent intake is required; sudden changes in vitamin K intake destabilise INR.
medication_interactions.recommendation: Patients on warfarin must maintain consistent vitamin K intake and inform their prescribing physician of any supplementation.

medication_interactions.effect: Calcium supplements can impair levothyroxine absorption when taken concurrently.
medication_interactions.recommendation: Take levothyroxine at least 4 hours apart from calcium supplements.

medication_interactions.effect: Iron supplements can bind to levothyroxine and reduce its absorption.
medication_interactions.recommendation: Take levothyroxine at least 4 hours apart from iron supplements.
references: Campbell NR, Hasinoff BB. Ann Pharmacother. 1992;26(12):1565-1566.

medication_interactions.effect: Magnesium forms chelation complexes with fluoroquinolone and tetracycline antibiotics, significantly reducing antibiotic absorption.
medication_interactions.recommendation: Separate magnesium supplementation from fluoroquinolone or tetracycline antibiotics by at least 2 hours.
references: Neuvonen PJ et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1990;47(2):140-146.
